data.description:

ACCEPTANCE: scroll works on mobile across the hero region; touch passthrough verified at 375px viewport.

RELATED: FRAC-109 (original fix, now regressed). Sibling tasks under FRAC-122 that touch the 3D hero: hero progressive animate-in, octahedron face textures.

Multiple sibling tasks under FRAC-122 may be in flight concurrently — if you find unfamiliar uncommitted changes or new files, INVESTIGATE first via 'git log' and 'lattice list --status in_progress'. Never revert/delete what you can't attribute.

data.description:
Andrew flagged Apr 8 2026 that clicking a flag from the home page opened the destination page scrolled to the bottom (scroll position transferred from the home page). User believes this was fixed in task_FRAC62 ('UX fixes: scroll-to-top, simplified navbar, no gray text, fix white space'). This task is to add regression tests so we don't ship a regression silently in the future.

TEST SCENARIOS:
- Home → click flag → destination loads at top (mobile 375px + desktop)
- Deep page → click another deep page link → loads at top
- Browser back/forward should preserve scroll where appropriate (don't break this with the fix)
- Mobile-specific: scroll-to-top works through the mobile menu drawer/full-screen menu

ACCEPTANCE: tests added to vitest suite; pass on master; cover at least the scenarios above.

RELATED: task_FRAC62 (the original fix being verified). Sibling tasks under FRAC-122.

WORKTREE PROTOCOL: Branch from master as frac-<id>-scroll-top-tests. Standalone — adds test files; minimal overlap risk.
data.title: Scroll-to-top on navigation: add regression tests

data.description:
Multiple impl sub-agents during FRAC-122 Track A and Track B reported the same 6 pre-existing test failures on clean origin/master:

- footer.test.tsx — 2 failures (look up '.italic' class on the branding div, but the className is 'select-none' on master)
- navigation.test.tsx — 3 failures (inner navbar related, possibly stale after FRAC-120/121 inner navbar cleanup)
- neighborhood.test.tsx — 1 failure (layout related)

These failures pre-date the FRAC-122 batch (verified by 5+ agents stashing diffs and re-running the suite — failures persist without any new changes). Each was investigated independently by FRAC-128, FRAC-130, FRAC-132, FRAC-133, FRAC-125 agents and confirmed unrelated to their work.

The orchestrator's 'npm test passes' gate is therefore softened to 'npm test passes for files I touched' until these are fixed. That gap will let real regressions slip if they happen to land in the same test files.

ACCEPTANCE: all 6 tests either pass or are explicitly marked .skip with a comment explaining why. Full 'npm test' should be a clean gate again.

Discovered: Apr 8 2026 during FRAC-122 implementation batch. Logged per CLAUDE.md 'recurring observations become tasks' rule (observed by 5+ consecutive agents).
data.title: Triage 6 pre-existing test failures on master

data.description:
USER FLAGGED HIGH PRIORITY (Apr 8 2026 evening): the mobile hamburger menu currently opens to a full-screen WebGL fractal model (the same OctahedronHero scene from the home page). User wants this replaced with a clean LIST of the section pages — much faster, more accessible, better mobile UX.

CURRENT BEHAVIOR (Navbar.tsx line ~316-349): mobile menu opens a fixed overlay containing only a Suspense-wrapped FractalCityScene. There's a close button, but no link list. Navigation only works by tapping nodes on the 3D model.

DESIRED BEHAVIOR: when the hamburger is tapped, the menu drawer shows a vertically-scrolling list of all 8 section pages (Story, Co-Living, Events, Campus, New Liberal Arts, Political Club, Lab, People), each as a tappable row that navigates and closes the menu. Visual style should match the existing mobile-first aesthetic (Jacquard letter glyphs + section names, generous tap targets at 44px+ baseline, the existing close button).

OUT OF SCOPE: don't touch the home page hero 3D model. Don't change desktop navbar. Don't touch the inner-page navbar.

FILES LIKELY TOUCHED: src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx (the mobile menu overlay block, lines 316-349). Possibly the imports — FractalCityScene/Suspense can be removed from Navbar.tsx if no longer needed there.

ACCEPTANCE: tap hamburger on a 375px viewport → see list of section pages → tap any item → navigates and closes menu. WebGL model no longer loads when opening the menu (perf win).
data.title: Mobile menu: replace WebGL model with simple list of section pages

data.description:
USER FLAGGED Apr 8 2026 evening. Two related banner issues:

LETTER BUGS (HouseBanner.tsx lines 130-134): the monogram letter is derived via '(house.displayName ?? house.name).charAt(0)' which gives wrong letters for any house whose displayName/name doesn't start with the right character:
- neighborhood: displayName 'Co-Living' → currently shows 'C', should be 'N'
- campus: no displayName, name 'The Campus' → currently shows 'T', should be 'C'
- lab: no displayName, name 'The Lab' → currently shows 'T', should be 'L'
- events: name 'Events' → 'E' (already correct)
- school: hardcoded 'LA' (already correct)
- forum: hardcoded 'PC' (already correct)

User explicitly named C→N (neighborhood) and T→C (campus). Lab also has the bug (T should be L) — fix all three at once since the underlying derivation is broken.

FIX: introduce a HOUSE_LETTERS map similar to the existing BANNER_IMAGES / ELEGANT_PAIRS maps in HouseBanner.tsx, with explicit letter for every house. Replace the inline conditional with HOUSE_LETTERS[house.id]. Keeps consistency with how other per-house data is managed.

TAGLINE COPY (src/data/houses.ts):
- events tagline (line ~193): 'Want to show up?' → 'Want to host?'
- campus tagline (line ~210): 'Want to host?' → 'Want to work together?'

Note: this changes the tagline that renders on the banner. The internal house metadata (description, name, route, slug) is unchanged. Per Andrew's earlier review feedback the wording was open — this is the user's chosen direction.

FILES TOUCHED: src/components/house/HouseBanner.tsx, src/data/houses.ts.

ACCEPTANCE: home page banner grid shows N for Co-Living, C for Campus, L for Lab; events banner displays 'Want to host?'; campus banner displays 'Want to work together?'. No regressions on other banner letters or taglines.
data.title: House banner letter bugs + tagline copy fixes

data.description:
USER FLAGGED Apr 8 2026 evening. The banner cards in HouseBannerGrid have 'hover:shadow-lg' (HouseBannerGrid.tsx line ~36), which adds a CSS box-shadow on hover. Box-shadow follows the element's rectangular bounding box, but the banners use clipPath polygon('0% 0%, 100% 0%, 100% 100%, 50% 85%, 0% 100%') for the pennant V-notch at the bottom. So the shadow renders as a rectangle that visibly extends past the clip-path V-notch — it looks misaligned and ugly.

USER'S DIRECTIVE: 'get rid of drop shadow on banners unless able to do at aligned with the cutout in the vector'.

TWO FIX OPTIONS:
1. (preferred if it works visually) Replace 'hover:shadow-lg' with a CSS filter drop-shadow which DOES respect clip-path: 'hover:[filter:drop-shadow(0_8px_12px_rgb(0_0_0/0.15))]'. CSS filter drop-shadow follows the visible shape, not the bounding box.
2. (fallback) Just remove 'hover:shadow-lg' entirely. No hover shadow at all — clean cards.

IMPLEMENTER SHOULD: try option 1 first (filter:drop-shadow), visually verify it follows the V-notch in dev mode, and ship it. If filter:drop-shadow doesn't render correctly because of the background image or the absolutely-positioned MandelbrotIcon overlapping the clip area, fall back to option 2 (remove the shadow entirely).

FILES TOUCHED: src/components/house/HouseBannerGrid.tsx (one className change).

ACCEPTANCE: hovering a banner card on the home page either shows a shadow that follows the V-notch shape (option 1) or shows no shadow at all (option 2). No more rectangular shadow extending past the V-notch.
data.title: House banner hover drop shadow doesn't align with V-notch clip-path

data.description:
USER FLAGGED Apr 8 2026 evening: the hover tooltips on the 3D model nav nodes (in the home page hero) flash open and closed multiple times when the cursor is over a node, instead of staying open as long as the cursor is on it.

ROOT CAUSE (OctahedronHero.tsx ~535-625, NavNodeMesh component):
- The tooltip is rendered via <Html distanceFactor={8} style={{ pointerEvents: 'auto' }}> on lines ~593-605
- The mesh's onPointerOver sets hovered=true, onPointerOut sets hovered=false
- Because the Html overlay has pointerEvents='auto', when the cursor moves over the popup itself, the underlying invisible hit-target mesh's pointer ray no longer connects → onPointerOut fires → hovered=false → popup hides → cursor is back over the mesh → onPointerOver fires → loop

This is a classic R3F Html-overlay-on-hover loop. The center 'Protocol' tooltip (line ~506) avoids this by using pointerEvents='none' — it can't be clicked but the tap handler on the underlying mesh navigates anyway.

FIX: change the NavNodeMesh tooltip's pointerEvents from 'auto' to 'none' and remove the now-dead onClick handler on the popup div. The mesh's tapHandlers (FRAC-124 tap-vs-drag discriminator on the invisible hit-target sphere, line ~609) ALREADY navigate on tap, so the tooltip's click handler is redundant — removing it doesn't lose functionality, it just stops the hover loop.

FILES TOUCHED: src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx (NavNodeMesh component, ~5 line change).

ACCEPTANCE: hovering a nav node on desktop shows the tooltip and KEEPS it shown until the cursor leaves the node. No flashing. Tap-to-navigate (both desktop click and mobile tap-then-tap reveal) still works exactly as before via the mesh's tap handler. Two-finger orbit on touch still works.
data.title: 3D nav node tooltip flashes open and close on hover

data.description:
USER FLAGGED HIGH PRIORITY (Apr 8 2026 late evening, after FRAC-124 merged): on real iPhone, both spin and scroll are still broken. Andrew originally asked to spin the fractal with one finger on mobile. FRAC-124 chose to prioritize one-finger scroll passthrough over spin (set OrbitControls ONE: TOUCH.NONE). Result: no one-finger spin path. AND apparently the scroll passthrough doesn't actually work on real iOS Safari either — user reports both broken.

ROOT TENSION: with one finger over a fullscreen 3D model, you can have EITHER scroll OR spin, not both. Two-finger orbit is technically possible (TWO: TOUCH.DOLLY_ROTATE in master) but undiscoverable on mobile. The user wants ONE finger to do both depending on intent.

PROPOSED SOLUTION: axis-based gesture detection. On pointerdown, record start (x, y, t). On first significant pointermove (>10px), classify:
- |dy| > |dx|: vertical drag → user wants to scroll → DO NOT setPointerCapture, let the browser handle the scroll natively
- |dx| > |dy|: horizontal drag → user wants to spin the model → call setPointerCapture, take over, rotate FractalObject's groupRef.current.rotation.y by accumulated dx * sensitivity, and preventDefault so the browser doesn't also try to scroll
- Tap (movement < 10px within 350ms): existing tap-vs-drag discriminator from FRAC-124 fires onTap → navigates

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Add a userRotationOffset to FractalObject (useRef<number>(0)). The existing useFrame auto-rotation adds delta * 0.12 to rotation.y. The new offset gets added on top: groupRef.current.rotation.y = autoRotation + userRotationOffset.current.
2. Add pointer event listeners on the Canvas wrapper div (FractalCityScene.tsx) that implement the axis-based classifier. On horizontal-drag classification, accumulate dx into userRotationOffset, setPointerCapture on the canvas, preventDefault.
3. Disable OrbitControls' two-finger TOUCH.DOLLY_ROTATE since we no longer need it (or keep it as a power-user fallback — user's call). Set ONE: TOUCH.NONE still (we manage one-finger ourselves).
4. The center 'Protocol' tap and nav node taps still go through the existing tap-vs-drag discriminator on the invisible hit-target meshes — those handle taps with movement <10px / time <350ms. Anything more is consumed by the axis classifier.
5. CRITICAL: real iPhone test before merge. DevTools touch mode does not faithfully reproduce iOS pointer capture and may falsely report success.

FILES TOUCHED: src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx (FractalObject groupRef rotation, expose user rotation ref), src/components/three/FractalCityScene.tsx (canvas wrapper pointer event handlers), maybe a new test file for the axis classifier unit logic.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- One-finger vertical drag over the hero scrolls the page past it (scroll works)
- One-finger horizontal drag over the hero spins the octahedron around its Y axis (spin works)
- One-finger quick tap on a nav node still navigates (existing FRAC-124 tap discriminator preserved)
- One-finger quick tap on the center octahedron still navigates to /the-protocol
- Two-finger orbit/dolly behavior unchanged or removed per user preference
- Real iPhone test: vertical swipe scrolls, horizontal swipe spins, tap navigates, no jankiness or pointer capture lockup
- Auto-rotation continues after user releases (don't lose the existing FractalObject useFrame rotation)
- Desktop click + hover behavior preserved

This is FRAC-124 V2 — a real fix, not the previous trade-off.
data.title: Mobile spin + scroll: axis-based gesture detection (one-finger does both)

data.description:
USER DIRECTION (Apr 8 2026 late night): the FRAC-124 fix didn't actually achieve scroll passthrough over the canvas on iOS — scroll was already dead in the canvas area, only the small sliver below the canvas (above the search bar) ever scrolled. Given that scroll over the canvas was never working, the simplest fix is to enable one-finger spin via OrbitControls and accept the trade-off.

ONE-LINE CHANGE: src/components/three/FractalCityScene.tsx, the OrbitControls touches config:
- Before: touches={{ ONE: TOUCH.NONE, TWO: TOUCH.DOLLY_ROTATE }}
- After: touches={{ ONE: TOUCH.ROTATE, TWO: TOUCH.DOLLY_ROTATE }}

That's it. Single property change.

EFFECT:
- One-finger drag over the canvas spins the model (Andrew's original ask, granted)
- Scroll over the canvas: still broken (no change from today)
- Scroll in the area below the canvas: still works (no change from today)

This is being merged as a SAFE simple-fix while FRAC-142's complex axis-detection PR (#106) sits open waiting for iPhone testing. If FRAC-142 turns out to work on real iPhone, it can supersede this. If not, this is the fallback we ship.

FILES TOUCHED: src/components/three/FractalCityScene.tsx (one property literal change).

ACCEPTANCE: one-finger drag on the home page hero spins the octahedron on mobile. Tap on a node still navigates. Tap on center still navigates. Two-finger orbit still works. No regression on desktop.
data.title: Enable one-finger spin on mobile (simple TOUCH.ROTATE — alternative to FRAC-142 axis detection)
Process Tyler's Apr 2026 website feedback

data.description: Piece A of a 4-task stack to fix homepage 3s cold-load problem. Paint something in the Canvas area during the ~900KB FractalCityScene chunk download instead of fallback={null}. Current state at src/components/sections/Hero.tsx:91 is — users stare at #faf8f5 background for the entire JS download. FIX: replace fallback with a static pre-rendered of the octahedron (PNG/WebP ~30KB), then crossfade to the real canvas when it mounts. WHY: PR #100 tried nested Suspense inside the Canvas and empirically failed per FRAC-127 review cycle 1 — r3f delays the first WebGL frame flush until all suspending children resolve, regardless of nesting. Painting at the DOM layer (outside Canvas) sidesteps r3f entirely. STACK: pairs with FRAC-127 (texture rewrite so post-chunk handoff is smooth), plus two sibling tasks for three-vendor chunk split and Netlify preload headers. CROSSFADE TIMING: fade on Canvas onCreated + rAF, NOT on Suspense resolution. Suspense will resolve the moment FractalCityScene chunk parses — which is before the first WebGL frame paints. Fading then would flash a blank canvas. ASSET PIPELINE: poster generation TBD at plan stage. Options: Puppeteer headless capture (auto-updates with scene), manual screenshot (drifts over time). Decide in plan.
data.title: Hero Suspense fallback: static octahedron poster + crossfade handoff

data.description:
Piece D of a 4-task stack to fix homepage 3s cold-load problem. Once the manualChunks task splits three-vendor out and injects modulepreload tags into index.html, layer on top: generate dist/_headers post-build with Link: </assets/FractalCityScene-HASH.js>; rel=preload; as=script entries for both three-vendor and FractalCityScene chunks.

WHY: these headers ship WITH the HTML response (not inside it), so the browser starts fetching chunks during the HTML download instead of after HTML parse. Saves another ~500ms on Slow 3G beyond what modulepreload alone buys.

BONUS: if project is on Netlify Pro, 103 Early Hints support doubles the win by sending the preload hints BEFORE the HTML response. Check plan status — free tier only gets regular headers.

DEPENDS ON: the sibling manualChunks task (piece B). Without the three-vendor split first, this can only preload the monolithic chunk, which is less effective (and nothing to stack with the modulepreload work).

STACK: pairs with poster task (A), manualChunks task (B, prerequisite), FRAC-127 (C/E — texture rewrite).

IMPL: post-build script reads dist/manifest.json, generates dist/_headers with the hashed filenames. Hashed names change every build, so this MUST be generated post-build, not committed statically. Alternative: use netlify.toml [[headers]] block with a plugin that rewrites on deploy.
data.title: Netlify _headers: preload FractalCityScene + three-vendor chunks via Link headers

data.description:
Piece B of a 4-task stack to fix homepage 3s cold-load problem. Current build at vite.config.ts has no manualChunks config, producing one monolithic 896KB FractalCityScene-*.js containing three.js + @react-three/fiber + @react-three/drei + scene code. The index chunk is 577KB.

FIX: (a) add build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks to split three + @react-three/* into a three-vendor chunk; (b) post-build step reads Vite manifest.json and injects <link rel=modulepreload> tags for the vendor + scene chunks into dist/index.html, so the browser starts fetching them during HTML parse instead of waiting for index-*.js to parse and execute the lazy() call in Hero.tsx.

WHY: saves ~1 RTT on Slow 3G by overlapping chunk download with React bootstrap. Also unblocks the sibling task for Netlify preload headers which needs the split chunks to target.

STACK: pairs with the poster task (piece A), FRAC-127 (texture rewrite, piece C/E), and the Netlify _headers task (piece D, which depends on this one).

RISKS: verify no circular chunk deps created by the split; verify HTTP/2 multiplex keeps multi-chunk overhead negligible. Validate with npm run build + npm run preview + DevTools network tab throttled to Slow 3G, comparing before/after first-meaningful-paint.

IMPL HINT: use Vite's build.manifest: true to emit manifest.json, then a post-build script (or small Vite plugin in vite.config.ts closeBundle hook) injects the hashed filenames into index.html.
data.title: Vite manualChunks: split three-vendor chunk + inject modulepreload into index.html

data.description:
Discovered Apr 9 2026 by FRAC-145 and FRAC-147 impl sub-agents (independently). The npm/pnpm typecheck script invokes tsc but tsc is not installed anywhere — neither in the main project's node_modules nor in agent worktrees. Both impl sub-agents fell back to relying on Vite's build-time TypeScript validation (via @vitejs/plugin-react) and skipped the typecheck gate.

WHY THIS MATTERS: Vite/esbuild does syntactic TS transpilation but does NOT do full type checking. Type errors that don't break syntax slip through silently. The intended verification gate is not actually running.

REPRO: 'pnpm run typecheck' from project root → 'sh: tsc: command not found'. Same in agent worktrees.

SCOPE: install typescript as a devDependency, confirm 'tsc --noEmit' passes against the current codebase, fix any type errors that surface, optionally add typecheck to the pre-commit hook chain. May surface latent type errors that have been silently shipping — fix or document them as part of this task's rework cycle.

DISCOVERED VIA: FRAC-145 PR #111 commit notes (Hero poster impl), FRAC-147 PR #112 commit notes (vite vendor chunk split). Both agents flagged it as a pre-existing project tooling gap, not a regression introduced by their work.

NOT A BLOCKER for FRAC-145 or FRAC-147 review/merge — those PRs validated via Vite build instead.
data.title: Restore typecheck: install typescript devDependency, verify tsc command works

data.description: User flagged during manual testing of FRAC-145 (hero poster crossfade) that the JPEG visibly flashes in on first paint, looking bad. Root cause: index.html has no preload link for hero-poster.jpg, so the browser doesnt start fetching it until React mounts and the img element renders. The image pops in late after a brief empty box. FIX: add preload link to index.html head. Works in both dev and prod. The FRAC-146 Netlify _headers preload only applies in prod. Single-line addition to index.html only.
data.title: Preload hero poster image to eliminate first-paint flash

data.description: After FRAC-149 added the preload link, user reports the image still pops up visibly on first paint. Root cause: the preload makes the JPEG cached faster, but the img element only renders WHEN React mounts (~200ms-2s after HTML parses on most connections). The page shows blank body, then HeroPoster suddenly appears. The pop is the React mount moment, not the JPEG load moment. FIX: add a static img tag directly to index.html body, positioned with inline CSS to match HeroPosters position. Browser displays this img the moment HTML parses. When React mounts, HeroPoster useEffect removes the boot img and Reacts own img (identically positioned) takes over with no visual change. On WebGL ready, Reacts img fades normally.
data.title: Render hero poster from static HTML body to eliminate React-mount delay

data.description:
Adopt the locked typography semantic taxonomy. Add 8 utility classes to src/index.css (.text-display, .text-title, .text-subtitle, .text-body, .text-body-lead, .text-eyebrow, .text-label, .text-meta), flip the global 'body { text-transform: uppercase }' rule so body defaults to normal-case, and add a Button 'sm' variant if missing. Update DESIGN.md with the new semantic type scale section.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md (mandatory read).

Why: Audit found 9 distinct text sizes, eyebrow weight drift (font-semibold/font-thin/unlabeled), 7+ pages duplicating a fake-button pattern, and 15 inline-style hero overrides. The codebase has no semantic vocabulary, so every component reinvents the same patterns inconsistently. This task creates the foundation; tasks 2-6 in the migration order depend on it.

Acceptance: 8 utilities defined; body uppercase rule removed; Button has default + sm variants; DESIGN.md typography section reflects the spec; existing pages still build (no visual regressions yet — that's the follow-up tasks).
data.title: Typography foundation — semantic utility classes + body case flip + DESIGN.md spec

data.description:
Replace ~15 inline-style hero overrides (

) with the .text-display utility. Sites: EventsPage, PeoplePage, StoryPage, LabPage, PoliticalClubPage, NeighborhoodPage, LiberalArts, BadgePlayground, Home, Navbar (hero contexts only — non-hero Jacquard uses stay inline). Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md. Why: Inline-style trio (fontWeight: 300, textTransform: 'uppercase', fontStyle: 'normal') is duplicated across ~15 sites — a maintenance trap and a missed reuse opportunity. .text-display encodes exactly this pattern. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation. Acceptance: Zero inline 'fontWeight: 300, textTransform: uppercase, fontStyle: normal' triples remain in src/. All hero text renders identically to pre-migration. Visual diff is null.
data.title: Hero display migration — adopt .text-display utility

data.description:
Migrate 7+ inline <a> 'fake button' patterns (border border-foreground/20 rounded-md px-8 py-5 text-sm tracking-widest uppercase bg-foreground/[0.03] hover:bg-foreground/10 + CornerDecorations) to the Button component. May require Button to support 'asChild' (Radix Slot) so an anchor can wrap it without nested interactives.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: The same button styling is duplicated across 7+ pages instead of consuming the Button component. The Button 'default' variant already renders this. Consolidation eliminates drift; future variant changes propagate automatically. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation (for any new size variants).

Acceptance: Every CTA on EventsPage, PeoplePage, PoliticalClubPage, NeighborhoodPage, LabPage, LiberalArts (and any others found) renders via <Button> or <Button asChild>. No inline duplicate styling remains. CornerDecorations behavior preserved.
data.title: Button consolidation — fake CTAs migrate to Button component

data.description:
Normalize all eyebrow/label/meta patterns to canonical 'text-sm font-medium tracking-widest uppercase' (delivered via .text-eyebrow / .text-label / .text-meta utilities). Eliminate the drift: ~12x text-xs vs ~8x text-sm, weights ranging across font-semibold, font-thin, unlabeled.

Per-site judgment: which sites are eyebrows (above a title), which are labels (forms/UI), which are meta (timestamps/credits/attribution). Spec captures the semantic distinction — visual rendering is identical for now.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: Eyebrow is the most-drifted pattern in the audit. Three sizes and three weights for what should be one chrome voice. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation.

Acceptance: All small uppercase mono labels go through .text-eyebrow, .text-label, or .text-meta. No remaining manual 'text-xs tracking-widest uppercase font-semibold' soup in src/. Plan file records per-site semantic assignments.
data.title: Eyebrow + label + meta normalization — adopt chrome utilities

data.description:
Reassign every <h1>-<h6> in src/ to either .text-title (text-3xl md:text-5xl) or .text-subtitle (text-xl md:text-2xl). Replace ad-hoc heading sizes that currently range from text-2xl (Campus) to text-6xl (PeopleDirectory) for the same semantic level.

Per-site judgment is the bulk of the work — e.g., Campus's 'Meet the Space' likely subtitle (relative to page hero), PeopleDirectory's 'People' is title. Plan file records all assignments.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: Same semantic heading level renders at wildly different sizes across pages. Creates inconsistent visual hierarchy. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation.

Acceptance: Every <h1>-<h6> uses .text-title or .text-subtitle (no ad-hoc text-* sizes on headings). Section structure on every page reviewed for title/subtitle correctness. Visual diff per page recorded in plan.
data.title: Heading size normalization — adopt .text-title and .text-subtitle

data.description:
After FRAC-51 flips body to default normal-case, the 37 explicit 'normal-case' class instances become redundant. Mechanical cleanup pass: remove redundant normal-case, adopt .text-body / .text-body-lead where appropriate.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: Dead class instances clutter className strings and obscure intent. Removing them prevents future agents from misinterpreting them as load-bearing. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation.

Acceptance: Zero 'normal-case' on body-tier elements (still allowed on headings that opt out of the italic Fraunces uppercase). .text-body / .text-body-lead adopted on substantial paragraph blocks. No visual regressions.
data.title: Body cleanup — remove redundant normal-case + adopt .text-body

data.description: FRAC-22 audit GAP resolution. New canonical utility: JBM weight 100 uppercase responsive text-sm md:text-base, normal tracking, relaxed leading. Body-length editorial / manifesto prose that carries chrome-tier mono-uppercase identity but reads as paragraphs. Migrate Home.tsx:35 Golden Age Protocol wrapper to use it. data.description: FRAC-25 local review surfaced inconsistent h2 rendering: 9 of 12 .text-title sites in Campus.tsx have 'normal-case' modifier, 3 don't — those render uppercase via the global h1-h6 cascade. The utilities lie about their rendering (DESIGN.md says mixed-case). Fix: drop 'text-transform: uppercase' from the global h1-h6 block (keep font-family, italic, tracking); add explicit 'text-transform: none' to .text-title and .text-subtitle.

data.description: Codifies two design rules from FRAC-25 review: quotes → .text-subtitle (visual contrast via display font); asides/bylines/role labels → new .text-aside utility (italic Inter body voice). Andrew Rose + Jake Zegil anecdotes migrate from .text-body to .text-subtitle. Nietzsche pull quote drops dead 'italic' modifier. 7 italic-aside sites (Want a reduced rate, Andrew/Jake/Nietzsche bylines, event host CTA, bio role labels, P.S.) migrate to .text-aside.

data.description: Container widths on the Campus page feel cramped — the body copy blocks that are left-aligned (the 'More than a WeWork' anecdote, the 'What's it like' section, the bio cluster, etc.) sit inside max-w-3xl which is constraining them more than necessary at md+ viewports. Combined with the outer px-6 md:px-[4.5%] padding on the section wrappers, the readable column ends up narrower than feels right. Scope: (1) investigate which constraint dominates at 375/768/1024/1280px — likely some combination of max-w-3xl on content blocks, max-w-7xl on section wrappers, and px-[4.5%] outer padding; (2) define a sitewide canonical max content width for left-aligned body prose (options: max-w-4xl, max-w-5xl, or custom) and codify in DESIGN.md; (3) audit + apply across Campus, Story, Lab, NLA, Political Club, Visit, Events, People, Protocol, Home — anywhere max-w-3xl is used on a left-aligned body block; (4) reduce section-wrapper side padding if that's also part of the issue. Out of scope: centered hero text widths (those follow a different rule). Mobile-first: verify 375px still reads correctly. Acceptance: consistent left-aligned body container width across all pages; canonical 'body container max-width' rule in DESIGN.md; no 375px regression. Slot in after FRAC-25 Campus apply lands — separate PR from typography/color migrations.
data.title: Reduce side margins and expand left-aligned body containers on Campus (likely sitewide)
